nodes.text:
-------------------------------
|          CHAPTER 12          |
-------------------------------


##You wake to the hum.##

A low, electric drone that vibrates through the floor, through the walls,
through the bones of your skull.# It is the first thing you are aware of,#
before the pain,# before the cold,# before the memory of what happened.##

You are lying on your side on a smooth metal floor.#
Your mouth tastes of copper and ozone.# Your head feels like it has been split open
and reassembled by someone who did not care about the instruction manual.##

$c<<Ow.>>#

$CYou open your eyes.##

Gray.# Everything is gray.#
Gray walls. Gray ceiling. A narrow rectangular space,# perhaps three meters by two,#
lit by a strip of sickly white light recessed into the ceiling.#
No windows. No furniture. Just the floor,# the walls,# and you.##

You sit up slowly, every muscle protesting,#
and that is when you see the barrier.##

Where a door should be, there is...# nothing.# An opening.#
But the air in that opening $Wshimmers$C,#
a faint translucent curtain of rippling light, like heat haze made solid.#
You reach toward it instinctively and—##

$WPAIN.$C#

A jolt of pure agony shoots up your arm, locking every muscle from wrist to shoulder.#
You stumble backward, gasping, cradling your hand against your chest.##

$c<<Force field.# I'm behind a force field.# In a cell.>>#

$CThe last thing you remember is the golden shimmer collapsing.# The vertigo.#
The blackness.##

And before that...# Theo carrying you.# Gray streets.# Blank faces.# Headbands.##

$c<<They caught us.>>$C

dialogues.precomment:
You drag yourself to the barrier and peer through the shimmer.#
The corridor beyond is narrow and featureless, lined with identical cells on both sides.#
And in the cells directly across from yours—
dialogues.line:
KRISS!# Kriss, you're awake!# Oh thank the stars you're awake!#
I thought you were dead! You were so still! You wouldn't move and I kept calling and calling—
dialogues.comment:
Smiurl's voice hits you from across the corridor, high and frantic.#
You can see him pressed against his own barrier, careful not to touch it,#
his skin flickering a rapid, panicked amber.

dialogues.line: I'm okay, Smiurl. I'm okay.
dialogues.comment: You are not okay.# But he does not need to know that.

dialogues.precomment: From the cell beside Smiurl's, Corolla's voice cuts through like a blade.
dialogues.line:
About time.# We've been stuck in these boxes for hours.#
They took our weapons. They took $Weverything$R.
dialogues.comment:
Her voice is controlled, but you can hear the fury simmering beneath every syllable.#
She is pacing.# Three steps forward, three steps back.# A caged predator.

dialogues.line: But when I get my hands free, I swear on my father's grave—
dialogues.comment: She stops herself.# Her jaw tightens.# She does not finish the sentence.

dialogues.precomment:
You look to the cell on your left.# Math is sitting cross-legged on the floor,#
his eyes closed, his face drawn and pale.# He looks like he has aged ten years.
dialogues.line: The shield... drained us.# Efeliah and I.# Our minds are...# quiet.
dialogues.comment: He opens his eyes.# They are bloodshot.

dialogues.line: I cannot reach beyond these walls. Not yet. I need time to recover.

dialogues.precomment:
Efeliah is in the cell beside yours.# You cannot see her directly,#
but her voice reaches you, thin and strained,# like silk stretched to its limit.
dialogues.line:
There are minds here.# So many minds.# But they are... dim.#
Smothered.# Like candle flames under glass.
dialogues.comment: A pause.# When she speaks again, there is pain in her voice that has nothing to do with the body.

dialogues.line:
They are the people of this city.# Hundreds of them.# Deeper in the complex.#
And they are... still in there.# Buried.# Screaming where no one can hear.
dialogues.comment: Her telepathic voice fades to a whisper.

dialogues.precomment:
You look to the cell at the far end of the row.# Theo.

He is standing.# Arms folded.# His back against the far wall,#
his massive frame perfectly still,# his eyes fixed on the shimmering barrier.#

He has not said a word.
dialogues.line: Theo? You alright?
dialogues.comment:
He does not answer.#
He does not even look at you.#
His eyes are moving.# Slowly.# Methodically.#
Following something you cannot see.##

$c<<He is watching.# Studying.>>#

$CHe has been watching since the moment he woke up.

nodes.text:
Time passes.# You have no way to measure it.#
Minutes or hours, swallowed by the hum and the gray light.##

And then you hear it.##

Footsteps.###

A single pair, this time.# Slow.# Metronomic.##

A figure rounds the corner of the corridor.##

A guard.#
A woman, perhaps thirty, in a gray uniform that hangs loose on her frame.#
Her face is...## blank.##

The same emptiness you saw in the canyon.# The same dead eyes.#
Across her forehead, the thin $Wmetal headband$C,#
its surface dull and featureless except for two small nodes at the temples
that pulse with a faint, rhythmic light.##

She walks the corridor slowly.# Methodically.#
Her eyes sweep each cell with the precision of a security camera,#
but there is no recognition in them.# No curiosity.# No threat assessment.#
Just the flat, mechanical scan of a machine performing a routine.##

She passes your cell.# Your eyes meet.##

Nothing.#
Less than nothing.#
You have looked into the eyes of animals with more presence.##

$c<<There is a person under there.# Or there was.>>#

$CShe continues to the end of the corridor, turns with mechanical precision,#
and walks back the way she came.# Gone.##

Silence returns.#
The hum fills the void.##

From Efeliah's cell, barely a whisper:#

$g<<There is a mind in there.# I can feel the outline of it.#
But the inside is...# static.# White noise where a person should be.>>$C#

Her telepathic voice cracks.#

$g<<Every one of them.# Every guard.# Every patrol.#
All of them still in there.# And no one can hear them.>>$C##

You look at Theo.##

For the first time since you woke up, his expression has changed.#
His jaw is set.# His eyes are hard.#
The calm, patient observation has been replaced by something colder.###

Anger.

dialogues.precomment:
Another guard passes.# Same dead march.# Same empty scan.#
This one is a boy.# Younger than you.

Theo watches him leave.# Then he speaks.# Quietly.
dialogues.line: I have a plan.
We're listening.

The barriers.# They connect to metal conduits in the walls.#
Follow the seams.# You can see where the current runs.
dialogues.comment:
He traces a line along his cell wall with one finger.#
You squint at your own wall.# He is right.#
Barely visible,# hairline grooves run from the barrier frame into the wall,#
converging on a thick metal strip that runs the length of the corridor ceiling.

dialogues.line: The headbands.# Same conduits.# Same current.
dialogues.comment: He pauses, as if that explains everything.# It does not.

dialogues.line: Theo, I love you, but I need more words than that.

dialogues.precomment:
The faintest flicker of something crosses Theo's face.#
It might be amusement. It is hard to tell.
dialogues.line:
The guard's headband carries current.# The conduit carries current.#
If I put one into the other...## short circuit.#
Barriers fall.

Wait wait wait!# You mean grab a guard?#
Through the barrier?! The barrier that $Whurts$Y?!
dialogues.comment: Smiurl's skin flickers a sickly green.

dialogues.line: Yes.
That's... that's insane! You'll be electrocuted! You'll die!!
Could.

dialogues.precomment:
Corolla has stopped pacing.# She is gripping the edge of her cell's doorframe,#
her knuckles white, standing as close to the barrier as she dares.
dialogues.line:
Theo.# If that current runs through you while you are holding the conduit...#
you would be the conductor.# Every volt goes through your body.
dialogues.comment: Her voice is very quiet.

dialogues.line: I know.
dialogues.comment:
He says it like he is confirming the weather.##

Silence fills the corridor.# The hum of the barriers seems louder now.

replies.line: Nod at Theo. If anyone can do this, it's him.
There has to be another way. This is suicide.

nodes.text:
You meet Theo's eyes through the shimmer.# You nod.# Once.##

He nods back.# That is all that is needed.##

Corolla exhales slowly through her nose.#
$R"Fine."$C# She says.# The word carries a weight that has nothing to do with agreement
and everything to do with trust.##

$Y"No!# This is not fine!# This is the opposite of fine!"$C Smiurl protests,#
his skin strobing between amber and a panicked gray-green.#
But even as he says it, he presses himself flat against the back of his cell,#
clearing space,# making room for what comes next.##

Theo unfolds his arms.#
He rolls his shoulders once.# Flexes his hands.#
Then he positions himself at the very edge of his cell's barrier,#
his right arm drawn back,#
his eyes locked on the corridor.##

Waiting.##

Footsteps.###

Distant, growing louder.# The mechanical drumbeat of a single pair of boots.#
Closer.# Closer.

$c"There has to be another way. This is suicide."$C#

Theo looks at you.# Patient.# Immovable.##

$B"No."$C##

Corolla shakes her head slowly.#
$R"He is right, Kriss. I have been over it a hundred times in my head.#
No tools. No weapons. No leverage.#
This is the only card we have."$C##

You look at Efeliah.# At Math.# Both of them are drained, their telepathy silenced.#
You look at Smiurl, who is trembling, his skin a mottled patchwork of fear.##

There is no other way.# You know it.# You hate it.# But you know it.##

$c<<If he dies doing this...>>#

$CYou don't finish the thought.##

Theo is already positioning himself at the edge of his barrier,#
his right arm drawn back,#
his eyes locked on the corridor.##

Waiting.##

Footsteps.###

Distant, growing louder.# The mechanical drumbeat of a single pair of boots.#
Closer.# Closer.

The guard rounds the corner.##

A man this time.# Broad-shouldered, slack-jawed, dead-eyed.#
The headband pulses at his temples.# He carries a short metal baton at his hip.#
His footsteps are perfectly even.# Mechanical.# Unstoppable.##

He passes Smiurl's cell.# Corolla's.# Yours.##

He reaches Theo's.##

Theo $Wmoves$C.###

One arm punches through the barrier.##

The sound that comes out of Theo is not human.#
A roar ripped from somewhere deeper than the lungs, deeper than the chest,#
a sound of pure, burning, defiant agony as the force field tears into his flesh.##

His hand closes around the guard's throat.##

The guard does not react.# Does not flinch.# Does not scream.#
His dead eyes stare at Theo as if observing a mildly unusual weather pattern.##

With one savage motion, Theo hauls the man forward,#
slamming the headband against the metal conduit strip above the barrier frame.###

$WLIGHT.$C###

A blinding arc of electricity erupts from the point of contact.#
Blue-white fire,# screaming through the conduit,# branching like lightning,#
following every seam,# every groove,# racing through the walls,# the ceiling,# the floor.##

Every barrier in the corridor $Wshrieks$C.#
A sound like tearing metal, like a thousand tuning forks shattering at once.#
The shimmering curtains flicker,# spasm,# and die.##

But Theo does not let go.##

The current is flowing $Wthrough$C him.#
You can see it.# His entire body is rigid,# every muscle locked,#
his teeth bared,# his eyes wide and white,#
blue sparks dancing across his skin,# his hair standing on end.##

His arm is still through the space where the barrier was.#
His hand is still clamped around the guard's throat.#
The guard has gone limp.# The headband is glowing cherry-red.##

$c<<LET GO!# THEO, LET GO!>>#

$CHe can't.# The current has him.# His muscles are not his own.##

And then,# with a sound like a gunshot,# the conduit blows.##

Darkness.# Absolute.# The ceiling light dies with the barriers.##

A heavy thud.##

Another.##

Silence.###

The hum is gone.

Emergency lighting flickers on.# Dim red, barely enough to see.##

You are on your feet before you know it,# stumbling through the dead barrier frame,#
your bare feet hitting cold corridor metal.#

Theo is lying face-down on the floor.#
The guard is crumpled against the far wall, motionless.##

You drop to your knees beside Theo.#

$c"Theo!"$C#

His skin is hot.# Not warm. $WHot$C.#
Like touching a radiator.# The smell of singed hair fills the corridor.#
Black marks trace branching lines up his forearm, across his shoulder,#
like a diagram of the current's path burned into his flesh.##

He is not moving.##

Corolla is beside you in an instant.#
Her hands are steady as she presses two fingers to his throat.#
Her face says everything her mouth will not.##

One second.# Two.# Three.###

$R"Pulse."$C#

She breathes the word like a prayer.##

Smiurl makes a sound that is half sob, half laugh.# His skin floods with golden light,#
bright enough to cast shadows in the red-lit corridor.##

Theo's eyes open.##

He blinks.# Once.# Twice.# Looks up at the cluster of faces above him.#
His lips crack into the faintest ghost of a smile.##

$B"Told you."$C##

Corolla makes a noise somewhere between a laugh and a sob,#
and punches him in the shoulder.# Hard.#

$R"Don't you $Wever$R do that again."$C#

Her eyes are bright.# She turns away before anyone can see them clearly.##

Math emerges from his cell, leaning heavily against the doorframe.#
Efeliah follows, one hand on the wall for support.# She looks at Theo and closes her eyes.#

$g<<You beautiful, stubborn, impossible man.>>$C#

Theo sits up.# Winces.# Looks at the burn marks on his arm with detached curiosity.##

$B"Could have been worse."$C##

$c<<He just electrocuted himself on purpose and his review is 'could have been worse.'#
I will never understand this man.>>#

$CThe corridor stretches ahead of you, dim and silent under the emergency lights.#
The barriers are dead.# Your cells are open.#
But you are still deep inside whatever this building is,#
and somewhere beyond the heavy door at the far end of the corridor,#
there are more cells.# More prisoners.#
More people buried alive behind static and silence.

You help Theo to his feet.# He sways once, steadies, and stands.#
The group moves cautiously down the corridor under the dim red emergency lights.#
The downed guard lies slumped against the wall behind you.#

Ahead, the corridor widens into a larger cellblock.#
More cells line both walls,# their barriers still $Wactive$C,#
still humming,# fed by a different circuit than the one Theo destroyed.#
Behind the shimmering curtains, you can see figures.# Motionless.# Staring.##

A metal storage alcove is set into the left wall.#
The corridor terminates at a heavy sealed door with a control panel beside it.


$D[Type an action at the prompt. Press TAB for help.]$C

actions.answer: The cellblock stretches before you under red emergency light. Active barriers hum in cells on both sides. There is a storage alcove to the left, a control panel by the far door, and the downed guard behind you.

objects.answer: Behind the active barriers, figures sit or stand in perfect stillness. Men and women, young and old. Each wears a metal headband. Each stares at nothing. One woman is mouthing words silently, over and over, like a prayer no one taught her to forget.

You trace the corridor walls carefully. The same metal conduit grooves run here, but these connect to a separate circuit — a control panel mounted beside the sealed door at the far end. It has a row of switches and a status display still glowing faint green.

The panel is simple — military-functional. A row of heavy switches labeled in faded text you cannot read, and a central override lever marked in red. The status display shows a grid of cell numbers, each glowing green. Active. All of them.

condition.refusal: I should look around more carefully first. There has to be a way to control these barriers.

objects.answer: The guard is unconscious, slumped at an awkward angle. The headband on his forehead is cracked, its temple nodes dark and dead. Beneath it, the skin is red and blistered. Up close, his face looks... younger than you thought. Softer. As if something has relaxed in the muscles now that the signal is gone.

You examine the cracked headband on the guard's forehead. It is warm to the touch, lightweight, with two small nodes at the temples connected by a thin metal strip. The craftsmanship is ancient — precise but worn. A tiny crystal is set into each node, one of them fractured from the surge.

The storage alcove contains standard-issue equipment: gray uniforms, restraint bands, and — buried under a pile of confiscated belongings — a medical kit. Old but intact, with bandages, salve, and a vial of something that smells sharply antiseptic.
The door is solid metal, sealed shut. A heavy-duty mechanism that will not yield to force. The control panel beside it seems to govern both the cell barriers and the door lock.

actions.answer: You are not sure what to use, or on what.

objects.answer:
You grip the red override lever and pull. It resists for a moment — then gives.

The effect is immediate. Every barrier in the cellblock dies at once, the shimmer collapsing like a held breath finally released. The status display flickers from green to dark, cell by cell, like stars going out. The sealed door at the far end clicks and swings ajar.

The hum is gone. Replaced by something almost worse: silence. And then, slowly, the sound of people stirring who have not moved of their own will in a very long time.

condition.refusal: I need to find whatever controls these barriers first.

actions.answer: You want to help, but you need to figure out how first.

condition.refusal: The barriers are still active. I need to find a way to shut them down first.

actions.answer: Nobody here is in any state to talk.

objects.answer: You call out to the figures behind the barriers. Nothing. Not a flinch. Not a blink. One man's lips are moving, but no sound comes out. Their eyes pass over you as if you are furniture.
The guard is unconscious. He does not respond.

actions.answer: You look around, but nothing useful catches your eye to take.

objects.answer: You take the guard's baton. It is heavier than it looks — solid metal with a rubberized grip. Not a sword, but better than bare hands.
You pry the cracked headband from the guard's forehead and pocket it. The crystal nodes are dark. Dead technology, for now. But Math might want to examine it.
You grab the medical kit from the storage alcove. Old but intact, with bandages, salve, and antiseptic.

actions.answer: Where? The only way forward is through the sealed door at the end.

condition.refusal: I am not leaving these people behind those barriers.

actions.answer: You stand in the dim red light, listening to the hum of active barriers and the sound of your own breathing. Somewhere deeper in the complex, something mechanical clicks at regular intervals. Time is not on your side.

dialogues.precomment:
The freed prisoners stir like sleepwalkers.# Most sit where they are,#
blinking,# confused,# the headbands dark and lifeless on their foreheads.#
Some pull at the bands with trembling hands.# Others simply stare.##

But one figure stands.##

He is wiry, medium height, with close-cropped hair and a jaw-length scar
that pulls the left side of his mouth into a permanent half-grimace.#
His eyes are sharp.# Alert.# $WHe is not wearing a headband.$C##

He steps out of his cell and looks at your group with the expression of a man
who has been waiting for something impossible and just watched it walk through the door.
dialogues.line: You.# You brought the barrier down.
dialogues.comment:
His voice is rough, cracked from disuse,#
but underneath the rasp there is something fierce.# Something unbroken.

dialogues.line: That was mostly him.
dialogues.comment:
You gesture at Theo, who is leaning against the wall,#
cradling his burned arm with quiet stoicism.

dialogues.precomment:
The man looks at Theo.# Takes in the burn marks.# The size of him.#
The fact that he is standing at all.
dialogues.line: Then he has my respect.# And my thanks.
dialogues.comment: He turns back to you.# Studies your face.

dialogues.line:
My name is Riff.# I lead what is left of the resistance in this city.#
Or I did, before they caught me.
dialogues.comment: He gestures at the confused, stumbling figures around him.

dialogues.line:
These people were free once.# Fighters.# Thinkers.# Anyone who would not bend.#
Joe took them.# Put the bands on them.# Added them to his collection.
dialogues.comment: The bitterness in his voice could etch glass.

dialogues.line: How many of you are left? Uncontrolled?
dialogues.comment:
Corolla has her arms crossed, measuring Riff the way she measures everything:#
quickly, thoroughly, and without sentiment.

dialogues.line:
A handful.# Hidden in the service tunnels beneath the city.#
We have been fighting Joe for...# I have lost track.# Months.# Years, maybe.#
Hard to tell in a city where the sky is a lie.
dialogues.comment: He looks at the ceiling.# As if he can see through it to the golden illusion above.

nodes.recap: Riff exposition about Joe and Projector

dialogues.line: Who is Joe? What is he using to do all this?

dialogues.precomment: Riff's jaw tightens.# The scar pulls white.
dialogues.line:
Joe.# Self-proclaimed Tyrant of Ayonn.# That is what he calls himself. Tyrant.#
Not king. Not leader.# $WTyrant$y.# As if it is something to be proud of.
dialogues.comment: He spits the word like poison.

dialogues.line:
He controls the city through a machine.# Ancient technology.#
It sits in the Municipal building — that ugly tower in the center of the city.#
They call it the $WProjector$y.

The Projector does two things.# It generates the golden shield over the city.#
The illusion.# The fear that keeps outsiders away.# And it generates the $Wsignal$y.#
The one that feeds those headbands.# The one that erases people.
dialogues.comment: He taps his own bare forehead.

dialogues.line:
Control the Projector,# control the headbands.#
Control the headbands,# control the city.#
Every guard, every soldier, every worker — all puppets.#
One man pulling the strings of an entire civilization.

dialogues.precomment:
Math has been listening with growing intensity,#
his bloodshot eyes suddenly alight with recognition.
dialogues.line:
A psychic amplification machine...# The Projector...#
This is the Master's work.
dialogues.comment: His voice is barely a whisper.# Reverent and horrified in equal measure.

dialogues.line:
The Memories speak of it.# A device built to shield a community,#
to project mental energy across great distances.#
But it was never meant for...# $Wthis$m.
dialogues.comment: He looks physically ill.

dialogues.precomment:
Efeliah has been sitting quietly, her hands pressed to her temples,#
her breathing slow and deliberate.# Recovering.#
When she speaks, her telepathic voice is stronger than before.# Not strong.# But present.
dialogues.line:
If the Projector generates the signal...# then the Projector can end it.#
Those minds are not destroyed.# They are $Wsuppressed$g.#
Disable the signal# and they wake up.# All of them.

That is what we have been trying to do.# For months.#
But the Municipal building is a fortress.#
Guards on every floor.# A psychic door on the top level that no weapon can open.#
We have thrown everything at it.#
Every assault.# Every scheme.# Every suicide run.
dialogues.comment: He looks down at his hands.# They are shaking.# Not with fear.# With exhaustion.

dialogues.line:
We lost good people.# A lot of good people.#
And every one we lost, Joe put a headband on and sent back against us.
dialogues.comment: His voice is steady.# But his eyes are not.

dialogues.precomment:
A heavy silence settles over the group.#
Corolla breaks it.
dialogues.line: You said a psychic door.# What does that mean?

Top floor of the Municipal building.# Before the Projector room.#
A door that has no lock, no handle, no mechanism.#
It opens to the $Wmind$y.# We have tried explosives.# Battering rams.# Fire.#
Nothing. It does not even scratch.
A psi-lock.# It requires a psychic key.# A mind of sufficient strength to match its frequency.
dialogues.comment: He looks at Efeliah.# She looks back.

dialogues.line: We can open it.# Together.# When we have recovered.
dialogues.comment:
She says it simply.# As a fact.# And Riff stares at her
as if she has just promised to move the sun.

dialogues.precomment:
Riff looks at your group.# Really looks.#
The giant with the burned arm who broke a force field with his bare hands.#
The red-haired woman with murder in her eyes.#
The telepath and the scholar, drained but unbroken.#
The glowing dwarf who is somehow still here.#
And you.# A stranger from nowhere who walked through a psychic shield.
dialogues.line: Who the hell are you people?
dialogues.comment:
For the first time, something cracks in his composure.#
Not despair.# Its opposite.##

Hope.

nodes.text:
Riff leads you through maintenance corridors no map would show.#
Duct-lined passages behind walls, beneath floors, through the guts of a city
that was never designed for secrecy but has been made to serve it.##

He moves with the practiced ease of a man who knows every bolt, every hatch,#
every camera blind spot by heart.# The freed prisoners follow in a stumbling line,#
some supported by others, the headbands removed and discarded like dead insects.##

Three turns, two ladders, one long tunnel through dripping darkness,#
and you emerge into warmth.##

A wide, low-ceilinged room.# Underground, clearly.# Concrete walls hung with salvaged blankets.#
Mismatched furniture.# A table covered in hand-drawn maps.#
Oil lanterns cast flickering orange light across the faces of a dozen people
who rise to their feet as Riff enters,# their expressions shifting from alarm to disbelief to joy.##

Rebels.# Survivors.# The last free minds in Ayonn.##

Riff grips the forearm of a woman who rushes forward,# her eyes brimming.#
He squeezes once.# Nods.# That is all they need.##

The room comes alive.##

Someone finds food.# Bread and dried meat and a thin, sharp broth that warms you from the inside out.#
You eat ravenously, barely tasting it, and it is the best thing you have ever put in your mouth.##

Efeliah sits beside Theo, her hands gentle on his burned arm,#
her brow furrowed in concentration as she channels what little psychic energy she has recovered
into easing the pain.# Theo watches her work with an expression of faint, grudging gratitude.##

In the corner, Smiurl has attracted a small cluster of rebel children.#
His skin is cycling through soft, gentle colors — lavender, warm gold, a shy seafoam green —
and the children are reaching out to touch his arm, giggling,#
pulling their hands back and then reaching again.#
He is telling them something, gesturing wildly.# They are rapt.#
For a moment, in the orange lamplight, he is not afraid.##

Corolla and Riff are bent over the map table, heads close together.#
She is pointing at something.# He is shaking his head.# She insists.#
He pauses.# Looks at her with narrowed eyes.# Then nods, slowly.#
Something has just been decided.##

Math sits apart, cross-legged, eyes closed.#
His lips move silently.# Reciting, perhaps.# The Master's Memories.#
Drawing strength from ancient words against a very modern darkness.##

You lean your back against the concrete wall.# The broth is warm in your belly.#
The lantern light is soft.# Around you, people who were strangers an hour ago
are sharing food, tending wounds, speaking in low voices.##

Tomorrow, you go to war against a tyrant and his machine.#
Tomorrow, you storm a fortress and break down a door that has no lock.#
Tomorrow, everything changes.##

But tonight,# just for tonight,# you are free.##

You close your eyes.##
